description: Add a three-phase rollout for loop step-back review, PRD trajectory checks, and project-local loop guidance configuration while preserving Ralph-style simplicity.

userStories.title: Phase 1 — Skill-level iteration review protocol
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the ralphi-loop skill to require a deterministic step-back review and PRD trajectory check so that iteration quality improves without runtime complexity.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/ralphi-loop/SKILL.md is updated with an explicit post-implementation review protocol.
The protocol requires re-grounding in prd.json and progress.txt, a code review pass, and a PRD trajectory check.
Progress logging format is updated to include review outcome and trajectory fields (for example ON_TRACK, RISK, DRIFT).
The phase remains prompt/skill-driven only with no runtime command changes required.
Existing loop usage still functions without requiring new files.
New tests verify expected behavior and output format assumptions where testable.
Tests, typecheck, and lint pass.
userStories.notes: Updated skills/ralphi-loop/SKILL.md with a deterministic post-implementation step-back review protocol (re-grounding, code review pass, trajectory check), expanded progress report template with Review Outcome and Trajectory fields, and added loop-skill-review-protocol tests to verify content/format expectations.

userStories.title: Phase 2 — Project-local configurable loop guidance
userStories.description: As a user, I want to define project-specific loop guidance in a local file so the agent consistently follows my review and direction preferences every iteration.
acceptanceCriteria: A project-local guidance file convention is introduced at .ralphi/loop-guidance.md.
Runtime loads and injects guidance when present during loop iterations.
If the file is missing, runtime behavior gracefully falls back to default loop behavior.
Guidance injection is scoped to loop-relevant phases and does not leak into unrelated phases.
A command set exists to show, set, and clear guidance without manual code edits.
Tests cover guidance-present, guidance-missing, and cleanup/session-switch behavior.
Tests, typecheck, and lint pass.
userStories.notes: Added project-local guidance support at .ralphi/loop-guidance.md with runtime injection for ralphi-loop-iteration only, graceful fallback when missing, new /ralphi-loop-guidance-show|set|clear commands, and loop-guidance unit tests covering present/missing/non-loop scope and clear+session-switch behavior.

userStories.title: Phase 3 — Optional advanced review-pass controls
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want optional review-pass controls so that teams can increase quality gates when needed without forcing extra overhead on all users.
acceptanceCriteria: Optional review-pass controls are defined (for example reviewPasses and trajectory guard behavior).
Default behavior keeps the current lightweight flow and advanced controls are opt-in.
If trajectory is flagged as DRIFT, loop output includes a corrective-plan signal and next-step guidance.
Completion behavior remains compatible with the existing ralphi_phase_done contract; any additions are optional.
Tests cover opt-in behavior and default-path backward compatibility.
Tests, typecheck, and lint pass.
userStories.notes: Added optional advanced review controls via .ralphi/loop-guidance.md front matter (reviewPasses, trajectoryGuard), extended ralphi_phase_done with additive optional review metadata fields, enforced opt-in review/DRIFT guards in runtime, emitted DRIFT corrective-plan guidance in loop output, and added loop-review-controls tests for opt-in gates + default backward compatibility.

userStories.title: Sequential rollout and release safety
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want strict phase sequencing and release gates so each phase lands safely before the next begins.
acceptanceCriteria: Phase sequencing is strict: Phase 2 starts only after Phase 1 acceptance is complete, and Phase 3 starts only after Phase 2 acceptance is complete.
Each phase has explicit go and no-go criteria plus rollback expectations.
Release notes and migration notes are defined for externally visible behavior changes.
Tests, typecheck, and lint pass.
userStories.notes: Documented strict phase sequencing with explicit go/no-go/rollback gates in tasks/prd-three-phase-loop-guidance-and-step-back-review.md, added release+migration notes to both PRD docs and README.md, and added markdown contract tests to prevent rollout-safety guidance regressions.
